text.title: AI-Native Product Development Operating Model Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-native-product-development methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every AI-feature PRD MUST cite an eval-set id and target metric thresholds before engineering begins.
rule.rationale: Without eval thresholds at spec time, 'done' is undefined and quality regresses silently.

rule.statement: Production prompts MUST live in source control with semver tags; rollouts go through PR review.
rule.rationale: Untracked prompt edits cause irreproducible behavior shifts; PR review is the same hygiene as code.

rule.statement: Every AI-feature rollout MUST pass a guardrail review (prompt-injection, PII leak, refusal, jailbreak) recorded as a sign-off artefact.

rationale.source: OWASP LLM Top-10 2026; internal incident review

rule.rationale: Skipping guardrail review is the dominant root cause of public AI incidents in 2025-2026.

rule.statement: Production AI features MUST emit BOTH model metrics (faithfulness, refusal, latency) AND product metrics (task success, retention) to one dashboard.
rule.rationale: Without dual-loop telemetry, model regressions hide behind product noise and vice-versa.

rule.statement: If product has no LLM features AND no plan to add any, skip and use vanilla product-ops methodology.
rule.rationale: AI-native overhead (evals, prompt versioning, guardrails) is wasted on non-AI products.
